sourceNotes: Historical-inspired, fictionalized for gameplay; does not use real party names in playable text.
Covers 2006-2010 Rajasthan events with expanded categories: corruption/scams, environment, welfare, health, and law & order.

Key anchors include Mahindra World City SEZ, NREGS roll-out, the massive Gurjar reservation protests led by Colonel Bainsla demanding ST status, police firings in Patoli and Bayana, the 2008 Jaipur blasts, the narrow victory of Congress in 2008 returning Ashok Gehlot to power, Barmer oil commercial production launch in 2009, Mount Abu eco-sensitive zone, and the late 2010 Gurjar agitations.

defaults.reactionRule: Each news item contains its own 3 active reactions plus no_comment. Reaction weights and effects vary by issue profile.
defaults.thirdPartyReactionRule: THIRD_PARTY can use opposition-compatible and neutral reactions.

newsItems.title: Special Economic Zone Cleared Near Jaipur
newsItems.description: The state government clears land approvals for the Mahindra World City Special Economic Zone (SEZ) near Jaipur, aiming to build IT and manufacturing parks.

reactionOptions.text: Fast-track infrastructure connections to the SEZ to attract global software and auto majors.

risk.badOutcome: Local farmer groups protest, claiming water diversion will dry up rural tube-wells.

reactionOptions.text: Demand that the SEZ developers reserve 50% of assembly and floor jobs for local district youth.

risk.badOutcome: Developers lobby warns that strict quotas will shift investments to other states.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a state-backed Bus Rapid Transit (BRTS) corridor to connect Jaipur city suburbs directly with the SEZ.

risk.badOutcome: High acquisition costs along highway sectors delay the BRTS launch.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: NREGS Launched in Six Backward Districts of Rajasthan
newsItems.description: The National Rural Employment Guarantee Scheme (NREGS) is launched in six backward districts of Rajasthan, providing 100 days of guaranteed manual wage labor.

reactionOptions.text: Speed up job card distribution in rural blocks and initiate desiltation works in community ponds.

risk.badOutcome: Delay in central fund releases causes wage payment backlogs at block offices.

reactionOptions.text: Demand that the state government expand the scheme to all districts immediately, citing widespread dryland unemployment.

risk.badOutcome: Central ministry states expansion will proceed in phases, limiting immediate impact.

reactionOptions.text: Propose mandatory social audits of NREGS works by local gram sabhas to prevent muster roll fraud.

risk.badOutcome: Panchayat secretaries strike, protesting against audit procedures.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Protests Over Limestone Mining Near Ranthambore Buffer Zone
newsItems.description: Environmentalists and local villagers stage protests against limestone mining leases granted near the Ranthambore Tiger Reserve buffer boundary, alleging threat to wildlife.

reactionOptions.text: Suspend all mining leases within 5 km of the reserve boundary and order a fresh satellite boundary audit.

risk.badOutcome: Mining labor unions strike, protesting against job losses in Sawai Madhopur.

reactionOptions.text: Lead a protestors' march to the state mining department office demanding permanent cancellation of leases.

risk.badOutcome: Industrial cement lobbies warning of factory shutdowns receives heavy press.

reactionOptions.text: Propose demarcating a statutory 10 km eco-sensitive buffer zone where only low-impact tourism is allowed.

risk.badOutcome: State cabinet delays buffer zone clearance, citing mining revenue commitments.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Severe Dust Storm Damages Mustard Crops in Alwar
newsItems.description: A severe dust storm and high winds lash northern districts of Alwar and Bharatpur, damaging harvested mustard and standing wheat crops.

reactionOptions.text: Announce an emergency crop loss compensation of Rs 2,000 per hectare for affected small-holders.

risk.badOutcome: Survey delays by local patwaris cause protests at district collector offices.

reactionOptions.text: Demand a complete waiver of land revenue taxes and electricity surcharge fees for the current season.

risk.badOutcome: State board states utility debt limits prevent power surcharge waivers.

reactionOptions.text: Propose setting up block-level weather warning cells to alert farmers prior to storms.

risk.badOutcome: Lack of weather radars in remote blocks delays warning messages.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Gurjar Agitation Blocks Delhi-Jaipur Railway Lines
newsItems.description: Gurjar reservation protests flare up. Activists block railway tracks and national highways in Dausa and Karauli, demanding Scheduled Tribe (ST) status.

reactionOptions.text: Deploy state armed police to keep railway tracks clear and offer talks with Colonel Bainsla.

risk.badOutcome: Clashes between police and protestors result in track blockades spreading to Bharatpur.

reactionOptions.text: Support the reservation demands, urging the government to pass a bill guaranteeing ST status.

risk.badOutcome: Meena community organizations launch counter-protests, threatening the party's base.

reactionOptions.text: Propose creating a new reservation sub-category for Nomadic and Pastoral communities within the state quota.

risk.badOutcome: Nomadic representatives boycotted the proposal, demanding full ST status.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Initial Round of Talks with Gurjar Leaders Ends in Deadlock
newsItems.description: Secretariat negotiations between Gurjar representatives and state cabinet ministers end in a deadlock. Protestors refuse to vacate highways.

reactionOptions.text: State that no negotiations will be held under coercion and warn that blockades will face legal action.

risk.badOutcome: Protestors block additional state highway sectors, isolating eastern districts.

reactionOptions.text: Demand the government appoint a neutral mediator and release all detained activists to restart talks.

risk.badOutcome: Ruling party accuses the opposition of trying to fuel social unrest.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a bipartisan assembly committee representing all communities to mediate the reservation dispute.

risk.badOutcome: Fierce rivalry between leaders prevents the committee from holding its first meet.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Rainwater Harvesting Mandate Enforced in Urban Housing
newsItems.description: The urban development authority enforces rainwater harvesting rules for all houses built on plots over 300 square yards, targeting water table recovery.

reactionOptions.text: Set a six-month compliance deadline, warning that municipal water connections will be cut for non-compliance.

risk.badOutcome: Plumbing equipment costs spike, generating complaints in middle-class wards.

reactionOptions.text: Demand that the state provide full financial subsidies to low-income households for RWH installations.

risk.badOutcome: Ruling party points out that the rule excludes low-income plots, reducing impact.

reactionOptions.text: Propose partnering with school science clubs to demonstrate rainwater harvesting designs in neighborhoods.

risk.badOutcome: Low resident turnout at local ward demonstrations slows down compliance.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Flash Floods Devastate Kawad Village in Desert Barmer
newsItems.description: Unprecedented torrential rainfall causes flash floods in the desert district of Barmer. Kawad village is submerged, forcing emergency evacuations by boat and helicopter.

reactionOptions.text: Deploy emergency disaster response teams, distribute food packets, and release immediate compensation to families.

risk.badOutcome: Relief distribution is delayed by sand dune road collapses, drawing local criticism.

reactionOptions.text: Demand the state request an emergency central relief package and waive local land revenue taxes.

risk.badOutcome: Central team disputes damage estimates in desert zones, delaying fund release.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a drainage master plan to construct concrete outfall channels to divert floodwaters safely.

risk.badOutcome: High costs of channel excavation in loose sand delay project execution.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Opposition Alleges Graft in Mahindra SEZ Land Allotments
newsItems.description: The opposition Congress holds a press conference, alleging financial irregularities and cronyism in prime land allotments for the Mahindra SEZ near Jaipur.

reactionOptions.text: Defend the allotments in assembly, releasing audit files showing compliance with single-window guidelines.

risk.badOutcome: Media publishes lease reports showing undervalued plots, damaging media image.

reactionOptions.text: Demand a CBI investigation and immediate suspension of the industry department head named in reports.

risk.badOutcome: Chamber of commerce warns that graft campaigns damage state investment climate.

reactionOptions.text: Propose that all future industrial leases be processed through an online public auction portal.

risk.badOutcome: Software errors in the auction portal delay subsequent project clearances.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Municipal Corporation Polls Scheduled in Kota and Udaipur
newsItems.description: Elections to municipal corporations in Kota and Udaipur are announced. Campaigns focus heavily on urban sewerage systems and electricity tariff rates.

reactionOptions.text: Campaign on the completion of sewerage networks and the launch of new city bus routes under state funds.

risk.badOutcome: Voters protest over delayed road restoration works after pipe laying.

reactionOptions.text: Highlight dug-up roads, delayed projects, and high property taxes in commercial wards.

risk.badOutcome: Ruling party wins Kota mayor seat due to strong local candidate backing.

reactionOptions.text: Propose launching digital grievance portals for each ward to resolve local civic issues in 7 days.

risk.badOutcome: Lack of staff in ward offices prevents timely resolution of digital complaints.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Bisalpur-Jaipur Water Pipeline Project Initiated
newsItems.description: The government lays the foundation stone for the Bisalpur-Jaipur drinking water pipeline project, aiming to transport 360 MLD of water to the parched capital.

reactionOptions.text: Allocate state infrastructure bonds and set a strict deadline to complete pipeline laying before summers.

risk.badOutcome: Farmers in Tonk protest, claiming pipeline diversion dries up local irrigation tanks.

reactionOptions.text: Demand that the government first secure Tonk's agricultural water needs before piping water to Jaipur.

risk.badOutcome: Jaipur residents criticize the opposition for blocking city drinking water project.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a formal treaty guaranteeing local villages 40% of Bisalpur water during low monsoon years.

risk.badOutcome: Negotiations delay pipeline contract awards by several months.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Government Waives Crop Loan Interest in Flood-hit Barmer
newsItems.description: To assist recovery in flood-devastated Kawad, the state government waives cooperative bank crop loan interest for all small-holders in Barmer district.

reactionOptions.text: Release matching funds to rural cooperative banks to restore credit lines for the upcoming winter sowing.

risk.badOutcome: Commercial banks refuse to extend credit, claiming rural defaults are high.

reactionOptions.text: Demand that the government waive the principal crop loans too, calling the interest waiver a token gesture.

risk.badOutcome: Finance department cites deficit limits, refusing principal waivers.

reactionOptions.text: Propose state subsidies for weather-indexed crop insurance to prevent defaults during climate extremes.

risk.badOutcome: Insurance enrollment is delayed by lack of digital land records in remote blocks.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Government Hosts NRI Summit 'Pravasi Rajasthanis' in Jaipur
newsItems.description: CM Vasundhara Raje hosts a high-profile summit 'Pravasi Rajasthanis' in Jaipur, aiming to secure investment pledges in heritage hotels and IT parks.

reactionOptions.text: Announce fast-track approvals and tax exemptions for NRI investments in heritage conservation.

risk.badOutcome: Local merchant groups protest, claiming NRI incentives create unfair competition.

reactionOptions.text: Demand that the government support local micro-industries first instead of offering tax cuts to NRI groups.

risk.badOutcome: Trade chambers criticize the opposition as anti-investment.

reactionOptions.text: Propose setting up an NRI-backed trust to fund drinking water pipeline projects in ancestral villages.

risk.badOutcome: Trust registration formalities delay project launches by a year.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Colonel Bainsla Calls State-wide Bandh Over ST Quota
newsItems.description: Gurjar leader Colonel Bainsla calls for a state-wide bandh, demanding that the Raje cabinet immediately recommend ST status to the central government.

reactionOptions.text: Deploy police escorts for national highways and appeal to Gurjar groups to join the Chopra committee hearings.

risk.badOutcome: Protestors block the Agra-Jaipur highway, disrupting cargo transport.

reactionOptions.text: Condemn the government's delay in sending the ST recommendation, holding the CM responsible for blockades.

risk.badOutcome: Meena community organizations warn that supporting the demand will damage relations.

reactionOptions.text: Propose setting up a reconciliation panel representing Gurjar and Meena elders to discuss a consensus quota.

risk.badOutcome: Rival group heads refuse to sit together on the panel, stalling the initiative.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Assembly Passes Rajasthan Religious Freedom Bill
newsItems.description: The Rajasthan Assembly passes the Religious Freedom Bill, prohibiting forcible religious conversions. Christian and Muslim groups raise concerns.

reactionOptions.text: Defend the bill as a measure to prevent fraudulent conversions of tribal families in southern districts.

risk.badOutcome: Minority organizations hold protest rallies in Jaipur, drawing critical press.

reactionOptions.text: Condemn the bill as a divisive and unconstitutional attempt to split tribal communities for vote-bank gains.

risk.badOutcome: Traditionalist voter groups shift away, accusing the party of minority appeasement.

reactionOptions.text: Propose that conversions be reviewed by a district magistrate-led panel instead of police, to avoid harassment.

risk.badOutcome: Legal experts warn that magistrate review is also unconstitutional, stalling the draft.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Madras HC, wait, High Court Stays Religious Freedom Bill Enforcement
newsItems.description: The Rajasthan High Court issues an interim stay on the enforcement of the Religious Freedom Bill, sending the draft to the Governor for presidential review.

reactionOptions.text: Comply with the stay order, directing local police to suspend active enforcement pending presidential reviews.

risk.badOutcome: Conservative organizations accuse the government of failing to defend the bill in court.

reactionOptions.text: Hail the stay and demand the permanent withdrawal of the bill, calling it a blow to communal harmony.

risk.badOutcome: Traditionalist groups launch pamphlet campaigns criticizing the opposition.

reactionOptions.text: Propose setting up voluntary all-faith peace councils in tribal zones to discuss social grievances.

risk.badOutcome: Rival religious outfits boycott the councils, continuing local friction.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Violent Clashes and Police Firing in Patoli Claim 15 Lives
newsItems.description: Gurjar protests turn violent in Patoli as demonstrators clash with police and block national highways. Police firing results in 15 deaths.

reactionOptions.text: Order a judicial inquiry, deploy extra paramilitary forces to secure tracks, and invite leaders for talks.

risk.badOutcome: Gurjar outfits block roads in adjacent districts, isolating Eastern Rajasthan.

reactionOptions.text: Condemn the police action as brutal, demand the Home Minister's resignation, and call a state shutdown.

risk.badOutcome: Commuter queues and local shutdown losses draw complaints from transport associations.

reactionOptions.text: Propose Rs 5 lakh ex-gratia for victims' families and set up a neutral administrative committee to hold talks.

risk.badOutcome: Protestors refuse talks until all detained activists are released without charge.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Meena Community Counter-Protests Raise Social Tensions
newsItems.description: Meena community organizations stage counter-rallies opposing Gurjar ST status demands, warning that sharing tribal quotas will dilute existing benefits.

reactionOptions.text: Appeal for social harmony, stating that the government will protect the interests of existing ST groups.

risk.badOutcome: Gurjar leaders accuse the CM of bias, resuming road blockades in Dausa.

reactionOptions.text: Accuse the ruling party of playing divide-and-rule politics, pitting two backward communities against each other.

risk.badOutcome: Opposition cadres in northern districts split along caste lines, weakening campaigns.

reactionOptions.text: Propose expanding the overall reservation pool and allocating separate sub-quotas to preserve peace.

risk.badOutcome: Supreme Court stays the proposal for exceeding 50% reservation limit.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Chopra Committee Set Up to Study Gurjar Quota Claims
newsItems.description: To defuse ongoing tensions, the state government appoints a committee headed by Jasraj Chopra to study the socio-economic status of Gurjars.

reactionOptions.text: Direct the committee to submit its report in three months and appeal to protestors to vacate tracks.

risk.badOutcome: Gurjar leaders reject the committee, calling it a delay tactic to avoid action.

reactionOptions.text: Demand that the state cabinet bypass the committee and pass a statutory bill recommending ST status.

risk.badOutcome: Existing ST organizations boycott the party's rallies in Mewar.

reactionOptions.text: Propose adding bipartisan MLA observers to the committee to ensure transparency in findings.

risk.badOutcome: Rival parties prioritize seat bargaining over panel observer roles.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Farmers in Sikar Protest Low Bajra Procurement Prices
newsItems.description: Farmer organizations block local grain markets in Sikar and Jhunjhunu, protesting against low bajra procurement prices and delayed state purchases.

reactionOptions.text: Announce a 10% hike in state advisory procurement prices and mandate cooperative cash clearances in 7 days.

risk.badOutcome: State warehouses report storage space depletion, delaying fresh purchases.

reactionOptions.text: Lead farmer dharnas outside collectorates, demanding the waiver of local market cess fees for the season.

risk.badOutcome: District councils report severe revenue losses, slowing down rural road repairs.

reactionOptions.text: Propose an automated escrow system linking grain sales directly to farmers' bank accounts.

risk.badOutcome: Technical integration with rural banks is delayed, leaving payments slow.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: SC Orders Removal of Commercial Resorts in Tiger Buffer Zones
newsItems.description: The Supreme Court directs the state government to demolish all unlicensed commercial resorts operating inside the buffer zones of Ranthambore and Sariska reserves.

reactionOptions.text: Enforce the demolitions strictly, stating that wildlife protection overrides commercial resort interests.

risk.badOutcome: Resort owners association files appeals, securing a stay on notices.

reactionOptions.text: Demand that the state demolish major corporate hotels first instead of targeting small local homestays.

risk.badOutcome: Local guides union complains that the stay delays tourism jobs, dulling issue.

reactionOptions.text: Propose eco-tourism guidelines that allow low-impact heritage hotels while protecting buffers.

risk.badOutcome: Drafting guidelines delays enforcement, allowing violations to remain active.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Water Contamination Tragedy at Chopasni School Claims 4 Lives
newsItems.description: A severe water contamination incident at a residential school in Chopasni, Jodhpur, results in the death of 4 students and hospitalizes over fifty, triggering anger.

reactionOptions.text: Order immediate suspension of the school administration, compensate families, and mandate school tank cleaning.

risk.badOutcome: Water supply departments report pipeline leaks near sewer lines, drawing bad press.

reactionOptions.text: Demand a judicial audit of all government and private residential school sanitation facilities in the state.

risk.badOutcome: Department officials cite lack of inspectors, delaying safety audits by months.

reactionOptions.text: Propose setting up parents-led sanitation committees in every school to check water quality monthly.

risk.badOutcome: Low parent attendance in initial meetings keeps the audit committees inactive.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Cairn Energy Begins Drilling Barmer Production Wells
newsItems.description: Cairn Energy begins drilling the commercial production wells in the Mangala oil field, Barmer. The project plans to start oil delivery by late next year.

reactionOptions.text: Fast-track land acquisitions and sign a memorandum of understanding (MoU) with central petroleum bodies for a local refinery.

risk.badOutcome: Environment ministries raise concerns about refinery emission impact on desert ecosystems.

reactionOptions.text: Demand that the state secure 40% royalty shares from central oil revenues before clearing refinery lands.

risk.badOutcome: Oil companies state that high royalty demands make the project unviable.

reactionOptions.text: Propose setting up a specialized petroleum university in Barmer under joint public-private funding.

risk.badOutcome: University senate approvals delay the syllabus launch by several terms.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Congress Launches 'Jan Akrosh Yatra' Against Raje Government
newsItems.description: Opposition leader Ashok Gehlot launches the 'Jan Akrosh Yatra' across Rajasthan, targeting the Raje government on law and order and Gurjar protests.

reactionOptions.text: Counter the opposition march by launching 'Suraj Sankalp' rallies highlighting single-window IT reforms.

risk.badOutcome: Rebel MLAs continue to hold private meetings demanding a cabinet reshuffle.

reactionOptions.text: Intensify the Jan Akrosh rallies in Eastern districts, focusing on police firing casualties in Patoli.

risk.badOutcome: Opposition parties fail to agree on joint candidates in upcoming by-polls.

reactionOptions.text: Propose establishing a code of campaign ethics to prevent personal attacks and focus on development.

risk.badOutcome: Rival parties ignore the code, focusing on caste mobilization.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Severe Cold Wave Damages Mustard Crops in North
newsItems.description: A severe cold wave and ground frost damage mustard and cumin crops in Sriganganagar, Hanumangarh, and Churu. Farmers demand emergency relief.

reactionOptions.text: Announce an emergency frost calamity compensation of Rs 3,000 per damaged hectare and waive land revenues.

risk.badOutcome: Survey delays by local patwaris cause protests at district collector offices.

reactionOptions.text: Demand a complete crop loan waiver and immediate power tariff cuts for tubewell pumping during winter.

risk.badOutcome: Utility board states debt limits prevent power tariff cuts, stalling the idea.

reactionOptions.text: Propose setting up block-level weather warning cells to alert farmers prior to cold waves.

risk.badOutcome: Lack of weather radars in remote blocks delays warning messages.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Chopra Committee Declines ST Recommendation; Advises Relief
newsItems.description: The Jasraj Chopra Committee submits its report, declining to recommend ST status for Gurjars but advising special development packages for their villages.

reactionOptions.text: Accept the report recommendations and announce a special package of Rs 280 crore for Gurjar-dominated blocks.

risk.badOutcome: Gurjar leaders reject the package, calling for a fresh state shutdown.

reactionOptions.text: Demand that the state government bypass the committee report and pass a statutory bill recommending ST status.

risk.badOutcome: Meena community organizations boycott the party's campaign in Mewar.

reactionOptions.text: Propose creating a new reservation sub-category for Nomadic and Pastoral communities within the state quota.

risk.badOutcome: Nomadic representatives boycotted the proposal, demanding full ST status.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Gurjars Launch 'Pilupura' Agitation; Block Agra Highway
newsItems.description: Gurjar groups led by Colonel Bainsla launch a fresh round of protests in Pilupura, blocking the Jaipur-Agra national highway and rail tracks.

reactionOptions.text: Deploy state armed police to keep railway tracks clear and offer talks with Colonel Bainsla.

risk.badOutcome: Clashes between police and protestors result in track blockades spreading to Bharatpur.

reactionOptions.text: Support the reservation demands, urging the government to pass a bill guaranteeing ST status.

risk.badOutcome: Meena community organizations launch counter-protests, threatening the party's base.

reactionOptions.text: Propose creating a new reservation sub-category for Nomadic and Pastoral communities within the state quota.

risk.badOutcome: Nomadic representatives boycotted the proposal, demanding full ST status.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Violent Clashes in Bayana Result in 20 Deaths
newsItems.description: Violent clashes between police and Gurjar protestors in Bayana, Bharatpur district, result in the death of 20 people. A high security alert is declared.

reactionOptions.text: Order a judicial inquiry, deploy extra paramilitary forces to secure tracks, and invite leaders for talks.

risk.badOutcome: Gurjar outfits block roads in adjacent districts, isolating Eastern Rajasthan.

reactionOptions.text: Condemn the police action as brutal, demand the Home Minister's resignation, and call a state shutdown.

risk.badOutcome: Commuter queues and local shutdown losses draw complaints from transport associations.

reactionOptions.text: Propose Rs 5 lakh ex-gratia for victims' families and set up a neutral administrative committee to hold talks.

risk.badOutcome: Protestors refuse talks until all detained activists are released without charge.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Serial Bomb Blasts Rock Jaipur; 70 Dead
newsItems.description: Eight serial bomb blasts rock Jaipur's crowded old city zones in fifteen minutes, claiming over 70 lives and injuring hundreds. A red alert is declared.

reactionOptions.text: Deploy state police, announce ex-gratia, and establish a dedicated Anti-Terrorism Squad (ATS) in Jaipur.

risk.badOutcome: Intelligence gaps lead to delayed arrests of key suspects, drawing critical press.

reactionOptions.text: Condemn the blasts as a major intelligence failure of the Raje government and demand central agency probe.

risk.badOutcome: Public views the criticism as political opportunism during a national tragedy.

reactionOptions.text: Propose installing a comprehensive CCTV and wireless security network across all gates of Jaipur old city.

risk.badOutcome: Technical setup delays keep the CCTV system offline for several months.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Government Signs Pact Offering 5% Special Quota to Gurjars
newsItems.description: The state government signs a pact with Gurjar leaders, proposing a new Special Backward Class (SBC) category offering 5% reservation, ending the Bayana protest.

reactionOptions.text: Draft a bill incorporating the SBC quota for immediate passage in the monsoon session of the assembly.

risk.badOutcome: Meena community organizations protest, claiming the SBC category will overlap benefits.

reactionOptions.text: Label the pact as an election compromise that will fail judicial scrutiny, demanding constitutional solutions.

risk.badOutcome: Gurjar voters welcome the reservation announcement, ignoring the legal debate.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a bipartisan committee of legal experts to check if the bill can survive Supreme Court scans.

risk.badOutcome: Fierce rivalry between leaders prevents the committee from agreeing on audit dates.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: First Translocated Tiger Released in Sariska Reserve
newsItems.description: To revive the extinct population, the forest department releases the first translocated tiger brought from Ranthambore into the Sariska Tiger Reserve.

reactionOptions.text: Inaugurate the release with media coverage highlighting the state's successful wildlife conservation efforts.

risk.badOutcome: Radio collar telemetry issues lead to the tiger wandering near local villages, creating panic.

reactionOptions.text: Demand that the government first release relocation funds for buffer villages before releasing more predators.

risk.badOutcome: Conservation groups criticize the party for prioritizing relocation disputes over tiger survival.

reactionOptions.text: Propose setting up cooperative eco-tourism guides in buffer villages to share tiger tracking profits.

risk.badOutcome: Licensing tests delay guide deployment, keeping villagers without jobs.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Assembly Passes Rajasthan Backward Classes Bill
newsItems.description: The Rajasthan Assembly passes a bill providing 5% reservation to Gurjars under SBC and 14% to economically backward classes, taking total reservation to 68%.

reactionOptions.text: Enforce the bill immediately and request the central government to place it under the Ninth Schedule of the constitution.

risk.badOutcome: General category organizations file immediate petitions in High Court challenging the bill.

reactionOptions.text: Label the bill as an unconstitutional eyewash designed to mislead voters before upcoming assembly polls.

risk.badOutcome: Gurjar organizations accuse the opposition of trying to block their reservation benefits.

reactionOptions.text: Propose an all-party committee of MLAs to draft a joint affidavit for defending the bill in court.

risk.badOutcome: Legislative delays prevent the committee from submitting its draft before polls.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: High Court Stays Implementation of new Reservation Bill
newsItems.description: The Rajasthan High Court stays the implementation of the new reservation bill, citing Supreme Court rulings on the 50% limit and lack of quantifiable data.

reactionOptions.text: Instruct the state advocate general to defend the bill, citing the special backward status of dryland families.

risk.badOutcome: Court stay remains active, drawing protests from Gurjar associations.

reactionOptions.text: Demand that the central government pass a constitutional amendment in Parliament to allow quotas beyond 50%.

risk.badOutcome: Central ministers delay action, claiming they are studying the legal impact.

reactionOptions.text: Propose shifting the budget to high-value scholarships for EBC students in lieu of direct reservations.

risk.badOutcome: Students organizations protest the shift, demanding guaranteed jobs.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Rajasthan Assembly Poll Dates Scheduled
newsItems.description: The Election Commission announces that the Rajasthan Legislative Assembly elections will be held on December 4, 2008, starting high-intensity campaigns.

reactionOptions.text: Campaign on the government's Suraj Sankalp development record, single-window reforms, and tiger revival projects.

risk.badOutcome: Local rebel candidates file nominations in 15 districts, splitting the vote.

reactionOptions.text: Highlight the reservation agitations, police firing deaths, and security failures during the Jaipur blasts.

risk.badOutcome: Seat-sharing adjustments trigger localized protests by disappointed candidates.

reactionOptions.text: Propose requesting the EC to webcast voting in sensitive booths in Eastern districts to prevent rigging.

risk.badOutcome: EC cites lack of internet lines in remote rural booths, stalling the webcasts.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Campaigns Reach Fever Pitch Across State
newsItems.description: Campaigns peak. Raje highlights industrial growth and the new reservation bill, while Gehlot promises stable governance, social security, and peace.

reactionOptions.text: Announce election promises to double monthly old-age pension benefits if voted to power.

risk.badOutcome: Urban voter forums criticize the promise as fiscally irresponsible.

reactionOptions.text: Focus the campaign entirely on incumbent failures in preventing the Jaipur blasts and managing agitations.

risk.badOutcome: Ruling party releases safety audits, claiming quick arrest of blast suspects.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a series of public debates on state highway upgrades and tourism investment plans.

risk.badOutcome: Opponents decline to attend, turning the debates into empty-chair PR stunts.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Congress Wins Narrow Majority; Ashok Gehlot Sworn In
newsItems.description: The Congress wins a narrow majority, securing 96 seats (supported by independents). Ashok Gehlot is sworn in as the Chief Minister of Rajasthan for his second term.

reactionOptions.text: Declare a social security agenda, launching immediate audits of NREGS works in rural blocks.

risk.badOutcome: Opposition claims the government is built on opportunism, demanding a floor test.

reactionOptions.text: Accept the mandate, reorganise the district party panels, and vow to audit the new government's spendings.

risk.badOutcome: Dissident leaders in the party hold private meetings, demanding leadership changes.

reactionOptions.text: Propose an all-party assembly bill to establish a state-wide drinking water pipeline grid.

risk.badOutcome: Utility cabinet disputes delay the drafting of the water grid bill.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Government Launches MGNREGA Social Audits in Rural Blocks
newsItems.description: The state government launches systematic social audits of all MGNREGA works in rural blocks, partnering with civil rights groups to check corruption.

reactionOptions.text: Publish all audit findings online and black-list corrupt rural contractors immediately.

risk.badOutcome: Panchayat secretaries strike, protesting against NGO involvement in audits.

reactionOptions.text: Demand a CBI investigation into muster roll fraud, claiming senior department heads are involved.

risk.badOutcome: Local labor unions complain that audits delay current week wages, drawing criticism.

reactionOptions.text: Propose introducing biometric attendance systems at all rural MGNREGA works to prevent fraud.

risk.badOutcome: Lack of cellular signals in desert blocks delays biometric uploads.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Cheap Wheat Scheme Expanded for BPL Families
newsItems.description: The state government expands the cheap food grain scheme, providing wheat at Rs 2 per kg under the public distribution system for BPL families.

reactionOptions.text: Expand the scheme to include all marginal farmers in drought-affected blocks.

risk.badOutcome: Grain procurement shortages lead to long queues at rural ration shops.

reactionOptions.text: Demand that the government distribute free cooking sugar and oil too, calling the cheap wheat partial.

risk.badOutcome: Rural families welcome the cheap wheat, ignoring the extra demand.

reactionOptions.text: Propose hand-over of ration shops to women self-help groups to reduce middleman corruption.

risk.badOutcome: SHGs report lack of storage facilities, slowing down grain deliveries.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Lok Sabha Campaigns Begin Across Rajasthan
newsItems.description: With the general election announced, campaigns peak. Congress highlights rural welfare audits, while BJP targets central inflation and security.

reactionOptions.text: Announce election promises to introduce a pension scheme for old-age small-holders if voted to power.

risk.badOutcome: Taxpayer forums write critical columns warning of deficit hikes.

reactionOptions.text: Campaign aggressively on delays in NREGS wage releases and the rise in food inflation.

risk.badOutcome: Ruling party releases audit logs, proving high NREGS employment rates.

reactionOptions.text: Propose drafting a joint water resources manifesto for the state to appeal to desert farmers.

risk.badOutcome: Differences on canal priorities delay the manifesto draft.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Mount Abu Declared Eco-Sensitive Zone by Union Ministry
newsItems.description: The Union Environment Ministry declares Mount Abu an eco-sensitive zone, halting all new commercial hotel constructions and mining leases in the region.

reactionOptions.text: Enforce the eco-zone guidelines strictly, setting up a state eco-monitoring cell in Sirohi district.

risk.badOutcome: Hotel and tourism associations strike, protesting against the ban on expansions.

reactionOptions.text: Demand that the state provide financial assistance to local hotel staff laid off due to project freezes.

risk.badOutcome: Eco-groups criticize the opposition for defending commercial lobbies.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a charter allowing low-impact home-stays run by local communities within the zone.

risk.badOutcome: Drafting the charter guidelines delays implementation, allowing violations to continue.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Congress Wins 20 Lok Sabha Seats in Rajasthan Landslide
newsItems.description: The Lok Sabha election results show a landslide victory for the Congress in Rajasthan, winning 20 out of 25 seats, while the BJP is reduced to 4 seats.

reactionOptions.text: Celebrate the victory, and negotiate central funds for the Barmer refinery project.

risk.badOutcome: Opposition claims the win was due to administrative bias and cash distribution.

reactionOptions.text: Reorganise the state party executive panels, focusing on building candidate bases in northern districts.

risk.badOutcome: Internal friction arises between senior leaders over responsibility for the defeat.

reactionOptions.text: Propose an all-party assembly bill to establish a state-wide drinking water pipeline grid.

risk.badOutcome: Utility cabinet disputes delay the drafting of the water grid bill.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Bisalpur-Jaipur Water Pipeline Completed; Water Reaches City
newsItems.description: The Bisalpur-Jaipur drinking water pipeline is successfully completed. Water reaches the municipal distribution lines in southern Jaipur, easing scarcity.

reactionOptions.text: Inaugurate the pipeline with extensive media coverage, highlighting the government's ability to deliver key projects.

risk.badOutcome: Minor pipeline leaks in industrial zones delay water delivery to homes.

reactionOptions.text: Demand a CAG audit of the pipeline construction costs, alleging financial irregularities in emergency pipe tenders.

risk.badOutcome: Public welcomes the regular water supply, ignoring the graft charges.

reactionOptions.text: Propose expanding the pipeline grid to rural villages along the pipeline route in Tonk district.

risk.badOutcome: High costs keep construction works slow in downstream blocks.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Severe Drought Declared in 26 Districts of Rajasthan
newsItems.description: Following prolonged rain deficits, the state government declares 26 out of 33 districts as drought-affected. Calamity relief works are initiated.

reactionOptions.text: Launch emergency rural desiltation works under NREGS and provide cash subsidies to cattle camps.

risk.badOutcome: Delayed wage releases cause protests at block offices in Western districts.

reactionOptions.text: Demand the central government send a team and release an emergency relief package of Rs 1,000 crore.

risk.badOutcome: Central team delays visit, leaving farmers to wait for relief.

reactionOptions.text: Propose providing 80% subsidies for drip irrigation systems to adapt to dry farming patterns.

risk.badOutcome: Farmers find system cost high even with subsidies, leading to low takeup.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Cairn Starts Commercial Oil Production from Barmer
newsItems.description: PM Manmohan Singh and CM Ashok Gehlot inaugurate commercial oil production from Cairn Energy's Mangala field in Barmer, marking a milestone.

reactionOptions.text: Promote investment in local oil field service industries and declare tax holidays for local setups.

risk.badOutcome: Local landholders file cases demanding higher value for refinery lands.

reactionOptions.text: Demand that the state sign a binding treaty with HPCL for the refinery before piping crude away.

risk.badOutcome: Refinery feasibility report updates delay the draft treaty by a year.

reactionOptions.text: Propose establishing a specialized petroleum university in Barmer under joint public-private funding.

risk.badOutcome: University senate approvals delay the syllabus launch by several terms.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Devastating Fire at Jaipur IOC Depot Claims 12 Lives
newsItems.description: A massive fire breaks out at the Indian Oil Corporation (IOC) depot terminal near Jaipur, burning for several days and forcing the evacuation of nearby zones.

reactionOptions.text: Deploy emergency teams, announce compensation, and order a state-wide safety audit of all fuel depots.

risk.badOutcome: Depot managers cite central jurisdiction, delaying safety audits by several months.

reactionOptions.text: Demand the immediate relocation of the fuel depot away from residential zones and stage protests.

risk.badOutcome: Relocation cost estimates are high, leaving the demand unfulfilled for years.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a joint board representing state pollution control and central oil safety officers to monitor depots.

risk.badOutcome: Inter-departmental coordination delays prevent board meetings, slowing progress.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Rural Teachers Protest Demanding Home Transfers
newsItems.description: Thousands of primary school teachers in rural blocks stage dharnas, demanding a transparent online transfer policy to move to their home districts.

reactionOptions.text: Launch a digital portal for merit-based transfers and process the first round of transfers online.

risk.badOutcome: Allegations of portal server glitches trigger protests in Jodhpur.

reactionOptions.text: Demand that teachers serving in desert blocks receive a 20% salary allowance hike as rural incentive.

risk.badOutcome: Finance department cites budget limits, refusing rural salary hikes.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a bipartisan committee of MLAs and teacher union representatives to draft new transfer guidelines.

risk.badOutcome: Political posturing prevents the committee from finalizing the guidelines.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: High Court Orders Closure of Balotra Dyeing Units
newsItems.description: The Rajasthan High Court directs the closure of all unlicensed textile dyeing units in Balotra that discharge chemical effluents into the Luni river.

reactionOptions.text: Implement the closures strictly and announce state loans for setting up advanced treatment plants.

risk.badOutcome: Textile workers unions strike, protesting against job losses in Balotra.

reactionOptions.text: Demand that the state government provide immediate monthly cash assistance to displaced textile workers.

risk.badOutcome: Finance department claims budget limits, refusing to issue cash payouts.

reactionOptions.text: Propose state subsidies for establishing Common Effluent Treatment Plants (CETPs) in textile zones.

risk.badOutcome: Construction of CETPs is delayed by land dispute clearances.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Janani Shishu Suraksha Welfare Scheme Launched
newsItems.description: The state government launches the Janani Shishu Suraksha scheme, providing free transport and medicines to pregnant women in rural government hospitals.

reactionOptions.text: Expand the scheme by providing free nutritional kits (dry fruits and soy) to mothers post-delivery.

risk.badOutcome: Substandard kit supply reports in two districts draw critical local press.

reactionOptions.text: Demand that state funds be spent upgrading rural primary health centers first, calling the kits a token.

risk.badOutcome: Rural families welcome the free transport, ignoring the health center critique.

reactionOptions.text: Propose training village midwives as certified health guides to assist rural home-deliveries.

risk.badOutcome: Medical associations protest the certification, citing safety concerns.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Municipal corporation elections in Jaipur and Jodhpur
newsItems.description: Elections to municipal corporations in Jaipur and Jodhpur are held. The Congress wins majorities, securing the mayoral posts.

reactionOptions.text: Announce special sewerage and road development grants for the new municipal councils.

risk.badOutcome: Local citizen forums protest over frequent water cuts during summer months.

reactionOptions.text: Highlight delayed road widening projects and high house taxes in commercial wards.

risk.badOutcome: Ruling party wins Jodhpur mayor seat, reducing opposition campaign impact.

reactionOptions.text: Propose launching digital grievance portals for each ward to resolve local civic issues in 7 days.

risk.badOutcome: Lack of staff in ward offices prevents timely resolution of digital complaints.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Frost Damage to Cumin and Coriander Crops in West
newsItems.description: Severe ground frost damages standing cumin and coriander crops in Jaisalmer and Barmer. Farmers demand state calamity relief.

reactionOptions.text: Announce an emergency compensation of Rs 4,000 per damaged hectare and waive current crop loan interest.

risk.badOutcome: Survey delays by local patwaris cause protests at district collector offices.

reactionOptions.text: Demand a complete crop loan waiver and immediate power tariff cuts for tubewell pumping during winter.

risk.badOutcome: Utility board states debt limits prevent power tariff cuts, stalling the idea.

reactionOptions.text: Propose setting up block-level weather warning cells to alert farmers prior to cold waves.

risk.badOutcome: Lack of weather radars in remote blocks delays warning messages.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Gurjars Call for Fresh Agitation Over Reservation Delay
newsItems.description: Alleging slow implementation of the proposed 5% SBC quota, Gurjar organizations call for a fresh mobilization of youth in Eastern districts.

reactionOptions.text: Promise to introduce a sub-classification model in the assembly and set up a state backward commission cell.

risk.badOutcome: Meena community organizations warn of counter-protests, opposing any ST quota split.

reactionOptions.text: Demand that the state government immediately issue the gazette notification of the 5% SBC quota.

risk.badOutcome: Existing ST organizations boycott the party's campaign in Mewar.

reactionOptions.text: Propose high-value educational scholarships for SBC students while waiting for court clearances.

risk.badOutcome: Students organizations protest the shift, demanding guaranteed jobs.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Government Schools Transition to CBSE Syllabus Format
newsItems.description: The state education department announces that all government secondary schools will transition to a curriculum matching the CBSE format, using NCERT books.

reactionOptions.text: Distribute free NCERT textbooks to all government school children on the first day of the term.

risk.badOutcome: Matriculation schools challenge the textbook content in court, seeking guide permissions.

reactionOptions.text: Expose instances of historical errors and praise of political leaders in the new textbooks, demanding revisions.

risk.badOutcome: Education department issues corrigendum slips, neutralizing the textbook controversy.

reactionOptions.text: Propose state training grants for teachers to adapt to the new curriculum formats.

risk.badOutcome: Training center schedules overlap with exam terms, reducing attendance.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Summer Scarcity: Water Trains Resumed to Pali
newsItems.description: Drinking water sources in Pali district dry up completely. The state government resumes running special water trains from Jodhpur to meet local needs.

reactionOptions.text: Allocate state infrastructure bonds and set a strict deadline to complete pipeline laying before summers.

risk.badOutcome: Farmers in Jodhpur protest, claiming pipeline diversion dries up local irrigation tanks.

reactionOptions.text: Demand the state government waive the municipal water cess for all households in drought-declared districts.

risk.badOutcome: Municipal corporations report severe revenue shortage, slowing down pipe repairs.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a massive project to clean and link historical stepwells to the city emergency supply lines.

risk.badOutcome: High contamination in stepwells requires intensive filtration, delaying supplies.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Cabinet Clears Proposal for New Rural Colleges
newsItems.description: The state cabinet clears a proposal to establish 15 new government degree colleges in rural blocks, aiming to improve access to higher education for youth.

reactionOptions.text: Allocate land in rural blocks and release initial budget grants to construct the college administrative blocks.

risk.badOutcome: Student unions in other cities protest, demanding university headquarters split.

reactionOptions.text: Demand that state funds be spent on rural polytechnics first instead of creating centralized colleges.

risk.badOutcome: Rural citizens welcome the colleges, criticizing the opposition's stance.

reactionOptions.text: Propose aligning the college curriculum with local industries to ensure direct placements.

risk.badOutcome: Private curriculum sync takes months of board approval, delaying syllabus launch.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Marble Mining Leases Stalled Over Forest Clearances
newsItems.description: Over 200 marble mining leases in Rajsamand are stalled following new guidelines from the state forest department demanding detailed wildlife impact clearances.

reactionOptions.text: Enforce the clearances strictly, stating that environmental protection is vital for Rajsamand ecosystems.

risk.badOutcome: Marble mining labor unions strike, protesting against job losses in Rajsamand.

reactionOptions.text: Demand that the state government provide immediate monthly cash assistance to displaced miners.

risk.badOutcome: Finance department claims budget limits, refusing to issue cash payouts.

reactionOptions.text: Propose state subsidies for conducting collective environmental audits to speed up clearances.

risk.badOutcome: Construction of audit zones is delayed by land dispute clearances.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: State-wide Campaign Against Child Marriage Launched
newsItems.description: The government launches a state-wide legal and social campaign against child marriages, targeting high-incidence blocks during Akshaya Tritiya.

reactionOptions.text: Enforce strict penalties on clerics, printers of invitation cards, and parents involved in child marriages.

risk.badOutcome: Rural community leaders protest, claiming the campaign harasses families during festivals.

reactionOptions.text: Demand that the government focus on girls' higher education subsidies, calling the penal campaign superficial.

risk.badOutcome: Educational department delays scholarship distributions, neutralizing the critique.

reactionOptions.text: Propose organizing voluntary community pledge programs in panchayats to delay marriages.

risk.badOutcome: Low volunteer participation in sensitive blocks keeps the programs inactive.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Hanumangarh Farmers Protest Indira Gandhi Canal Pollution
newsItems.description: Farmers in Hanumangarh stage protests, alleging that chemical and industrial waste discharged from Punjab factories is contaminating Indira Gandhi Canal water.

reactionOptions.text: Send a high-level ministerial delegation to Punjab to demand immediate sealing of polluting factory lines.

risk.badOutcome: Punjab factories dispute the pollution source, delaying any corrective action.

reactionOptions.text: Lead a farmers' border sit-in, demanding that the central government intervene to stop water contamination.

risk.badOutcome: State border police arrests protestors to prevent inter-state clashes.

reactionOptions.text: Propose constructing water treatment plants at canal entry points to filter water before municipal distribution.

risk.badOutcome: High construction costs of treatment plants delay the project by a year.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Gurjars Resume Agitation in Pilupura; Block Rail Tracks
newsItems.description: Gurjar protestors block railway tracks in Pilupura, Bharatpur district, demanding the immediate implementation of the 5% reservation under SBC.

reactionOptions.text: Deploy state armed police to keep railway tracks clear and offer talks with Colonel Bainsla.

risk.badOutcome: Clashes between police and protestors result in track blockades spreading to Dausa.

reactionOptions.text: Support the reservation demands, urging the government to pass a bill guaranteeing SBC status.

risk.badOutcome: Meena community organizations launch counter-protests, threatening the party's base.

reactionOptions.text: Propose creating a new reservation sub-category for Nomadic and Pastoral communities within the state quota.

risk.badOutcome: Nomadic representatives boycotted the proposal, demanding full ST status.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Government Initiates Talks with Colonel Bainsla in Bayana
newsItems.description: The state government sends a senior ministerial team to hold talks with Gurjar leader Colonel Bainsla in Bayana to resolve the railway blockade.

reactionOptions.text: Promise to introduce a sub-classification model in the assembly and set up a state backward commission cell.

risk.badOutcome: Meena community organizations warn of counter-protests, opposing any ST quota split.

reactionOptions.text: Demand that the state government immediately issue the gazette notification of the 5% SBC quota.

risk.badOutcome: Existing ST organizations boycott the party's campaign in Mewar.

reactionOptions.text: Propose high-value educational scholarships for SBC students while waiting for court clearances.

risk.badOutcome: Students organizations protest the shift, demanding guaranteed jobs.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Unseasonal Winter Rains Damage standing Crops in East
newsItems.description: Unseasonal heavy winter rains damage standing mustard and wheat crops in Bharatpur and Alwar districts. Farmers demand immediate relief.

reactionOptions.text: Announce an emergency compensation of Rs 10,000 per damaged acre and waive current crop loans.

risk.badOutcome: Revenue surveyors take weeks to measure damaged plots, stalling payouts.

reactionOptions.text: Demand the central government send a team and release an emergency relief package of Rs 500 crore.

risk.badOutcome: Central team delays visit, leaving farmers to wait for relief.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a long-term engineering project to raise and reinforce embankments along feeder canals.

risk.badOutcome: High project cost keeps construction works slow in downstream blocks.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.
